business.headline: Government rules out fuel price hike after assembly polls conclude tomorrowDespite soaring global crude prices and heavy losses for fuel retailers, the government rules out any immediate hike in petrol and diesel rates, even as panic buying and supply concerns emerge in some states.

UAE exits oil production groups OPEC, OPEC+ amid global energy crisisThe decision, confirmed on Tuesday, delivers a big setback to the oil-producing alliance and its de facto leader, Saudi Arabia. The UAE, long seen as a major and reliable member, stepping away now risks weakening coordination within the bloc.

From power bills to food prices: How the heatwave is hitting your walletA heatwave is wreaking havoc across India, and it is starting to hit your wallet, pushing up electricity bills, food prices and everyday expenses through rising demand and supply disruptions.

Sensex, Nifty slip amid RBI norms, global cues; banks lead declineBenchmark indices ended lower on Wednesday as banking stocks fell after the RBI finalised key lending norms. Caution ahead of the US Fed decision, weak Asian cues and elevated oil prices kept investors on edge.

Are India's IT giants struggling to keep pace with global AI boom?The sector that defined India's growth story is entering a phase of uncertainty. India's biggest wealth creator is no longer the market's safest bet. The IT story isn't over, but the rules of the game have clearly changed.

Airlines send fuel price SOS to government, warn of shutdown in operationsIn a letter to the Civil Aviation Ministry, the Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA), which represents carriers such as Air India, IndiGo and SpiceJet, warned that the sector is facing "extreme stress" due to soaring aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices.

Snabbit raises $56 million as instant home services race heats upSnabbit has raised $56 million to expand its instant home services business across India. The round underlines growing investor confidence in organising a vast but still informal market.

Got this HR email on salary reimbursements? It could help you save more taxIf you have recently received an email from your office's HR department on salary reimbursements, it may be worth a closer look. It could influence how part of your salary is taxed.

Crude oil at $110: Why it is a wake-up call for India and the worldCrude oil has surged towards $110 a barrel as markets react to war-linked supply fears in West Asia. The rise matters for India and beyond because it can feed into fuel, inflation, imports and household costs.

Up nearly 40% in 2 days: What's fuelling this pharma stock's rallyCohance Lifesciences shares surged after the company named Umang Vohra as Executive Chairman and Group CEO. The move lifted expectations of faster growth, stronger execution and bigger scale in its CDMO business.

Six brokerages, six buy ratings: The story that market is missing about PaytmTarget prices set by Bernstein, Goldman Sachs, Jefferies, Bank of America, Investec and Emkay Global range from 1,250 to 1,500, pointing to an upside of as much as 31% from its Monday close of 1,138.
8th Pay Commission website down as key Delhi meetings set to beginThe 8th Pay Commission website appeared inaccessible as consultations with employee unions were set to begin in Delhi.

8th Pay Commission: Will govt employee salaries rise as key talks begin today?The 8th Pay Commission will begin a three-day consultation with employee unions in New Delhi. The talks will shape decisions on pay, allowances, pensions and the closely watched fitment factor.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as rising crude oil prices hamper investor sentimentThe BSE Sensex was trading at 77,294.03, down 9.60 points or 0.01% at around 9:41 am. The NSE Nifty 50 stood at 24,127.90, up 35.20 points or 0.15%.

Nomura sees India's growth slowing early in FY27 before a later reboundNomura said India's growth could slow in the first half of FY27 before improving later in the year. The report said steady demand and policy support may aid recovery, though oil and global risks remain.

8th Pay Commission: From arrears to 3.83 fitment, what postal staff wantThe proposal has been submitted by the Federation of National Postal Organisations (FNPO) ahead of the Commission's consultation meetings scheduled in New Delhi from April 28 to 30.

Fertiliser prices surge globally amid Hormuz crisis. Will farmers be impacted?Currently, India has about 19.02 million tonnes of fertilisers in stock, compared to a requirement of 39.05 million tonnes for the kharif season.

UP rolls out 10-year 'warranty' for rural water schemesUttar Pradesh has made a 10-year maintenance period mandatory for rural drinking water projects under Jal Arpan. The shift aims to keep village schemes running longer through accountability, quality checks and lower power costs.

Oil India discovers oil, gas in Libya amid global energy tensionsThe state-owned firm informed stock exchanges that the discovery was made in the Area 95/96 block in Libya, located in the Ghadames Basin in the southwestern region of the country. The area is considered highly prospective for hydrocarbons.

Reddit user asks where to invest Rs 42 lakh. We got experts to answerA Reddit user recently asked a question many retirees quietly worry about, i.e., what to do with Rs 42 lakh after retirement. Should it stay safe in fixed deposits or be moved into mutual funds for better growth? Let's have a look.

entertainment.headline: Neha Dhupia, Angad Bedi share how unplanned pregnancy changed their love story Neha Dhupia and Angad Bedi opened up about their unconventional love story and revealed how an unplanned pregnancy changed their relationship. The couple shared that their daughter Mehr became the reason they decided to get married in 2018.

Sonali Bendre on battling stage 4 cancer: Early detection could have made difference Sonali Bendre reflected on her stage 4 cancer battle and said early detection could have spared her from the hardship she endured.

Bigg Boss Kannada's Rajat Kishan allegedly films explosion on road, sparks outrage Former Bigg Boss Kannada contestant Rajat Kishan has sparked outrage on social media after allegedly filming a reel showing a petrol bomb-like explosion on a public road. The viral clip has raised public safety concerns.

Michael, Hail Mary, The Mummy, Super Mario score in India despite Dhurandhar 2 Despite the overwhelming success of Dhurandhar 2, four Hollywood films have scored at the Indian box office in the past months, drawing the audience with original and engaging content.

Atlee to use body double for Deepika in Raaka after pregnancy reveal: Report Reports say Deepika Padukone's role in Atlee's Raaka remains unchanged after her second pregnancy reveal, with a body double set for select action scenes. The film is expected to release in theatres in 2027.

Mohanlal marks 38th anniversary with wife Suchitra: Feels like the beginning Mohanlal marked his 38th wedding anniversary with wife Suchitra by sharing a heartfelt post, featuring the Statue of Liberty in the backdrop. The actor's romantic note quickly won over fans on social media.

Dhanush's son Yathra to make acting debut soon? Actor breaks silence Dhanush said at the Kara pre-release event that it is too early to say if Yathra will act in films. His remark comes amid launch rumours even as Yathra has already been credited on a song in Nilavuku En Mel Ennadi Kobam.

Watch: Akshay Kumar dances in heels after prank payback on Wheel of Fortune finale A Wheel of Fortune finale clip showed Akshay Kumar taking Jacqueline Fernandez's high-heels dance challenge as prank payback. The playful moment went viral and drew wide attention across social media.

Khalnayak Returns: Subhash Ghai on Sanjay Dutt's note from jail, why he won't direct Subhash Ghai explained why he won't direct Khalnayak Returns and recalled Sanjay Dutt writing to him from jail, expressing his wish to make a sequel.

Anubhav Sinha wants to make a horror film though he's scared of the genre Anubhav Sinha said he still chooses films on his own terms and wants to make a horror film next. His remarks highlighted both his creative independence and his fascination with a genre that still scares him.

Asha Bhosle's grandson Chin2 recalls her curiosity for AI, passion for theatre Asha Bhosle's grandson Chintu Bhosle remembered her playful nature at home and said her curiosity about AI, fearless attitude, and love for the arts stayed strong till her final years.

Harry Styles-Zoe Kravitz engagement buzz: Here's what insiders say Harry Styles and Zoe Kravitz have reportedly shared their engagement news with close friends. Ring sightings in London and recent reports have intensified speculation, though the couple has not confirmed it.

George Clooney calls for healing amid political tensions: No place for violence George Clooney used his Chaplin Award speech to denounce political violence and call for unity. His remarks, along with references to Edward R. Murrow and CBS, turned the gala into a wider reflection on public life and the media.

Jee Le Zaraa: Here's what director Farhan Akhtar said on uncertainty around film Farhan Akhtar has addressed the speculation around delays and possible recasting for Jee Le Zaraa. His remarks keep the film's status open even as past reports said scheduling issues had been resolved.

Django/Zorro film is on, based on crossover comic by Quentin Tarantino-Matt Wagner A new Django and Zorro crossover film with LA Confidential writer Brian Helgeland is in the works. It is inspired by the 2014 comic series co-written by Quentin Tarantino and Matt Wagner.

Bigg Boss Telugu fame Ashu Reddy accused of Rs 9.35-crore marriage fraud Hyderabad police have registered a cheating case against actor Ashu Reddy over allegations linked to a marriage promise and Rs 9.35 crore. Ashu Reddy has denied wrongdoing, while investigators said the enquiry is continuing.
Bobby Deol's Bandar postponed to June, avoids May box office clash Bobby Deol's Bandar has been pushed to June. The new date keeps it clear of two May releases and places it a day after Yash's Toxic.

Rashmika Mandanna returns as presenter at Anime Awards 2026, joins The Weeknd Rashmika Mandanna will return as a presenter at the 10th Global Anime Awards in Tokyo on May 23, where The Weeknd will also join the line-up. Her return follows her 2024 milestone as the first Indian presenter at the ceremony.

Aneet Padda's viral Shakti Shalini glimpse sparks buzz over her new avatar Aneet Padda's viral Shakti Shalini set video shows her in character as a Rajasthani schoolgirl. The glimpse has stirred curiosity about her looks and the film's place in Maddock's horror comedy universe.

general news.headline: EC swings into action after row over UP Singham's remarks against TMC candidateAn EC-ordered transfer, a viral 'Singham' warning, protests, and a serious complaint alleging night raids - the Falta controversy deepens ahead of April 29 polling in West Bengal.

The UAE, long seen as a major and reliable member, stepping away now risks weakening coordination within the bloc.

Government rules out fuel price hike after assembly polls conclude tomorrowDespite soaring global crude prices and heavy losses for fuel retailers, the government rules out any immediate hike in petrol and diesel rates, even as panic buying and supply concerns emerge in some states.

'AAPda' for AAP after Gujarat civic poll drubbing, exodus of MPs, Delhi lossThe AAP, widely seen as the principal opposition to the BJP, suffered a massive setback in the Gujarat local body polls. The result, which erodes the party's gains from 2021, comes as a double whammy for the AAP days after it was dealt a blow by the exodus of seven Rajya Sabha MPs to the BJP.

Is 'peace mediator' Pakistan looking for exit by attacking Afghanistan?Pakistan fired mortars and missiles into Afghanistan's Kunar province, hitting a university, after three weeks of ceasefire. The strikes came as Islamabad's effort to mediate between the US and Iran stalled. Is Pakistan desperately looking for an exit route?

If he's 'Singham', I'm 'Pushpa': TMC leader fires back after UP cop's warningA fiery 'Singham vs Pushpa' remark sets off a political storm in Bengal as protests erupt, an IPS officer faces accusations, and tensions rise ahead of the crucial second phase of polling.

Didn't demand physical presence: Bank after Odisha man brings sister's skeletonThe man, identified as Jeetu Munda (50) of Dianali village, was seeking to withdraw Rs 20,000 from the bank account of his elder sister, Kalra Munda (56), who died on January 26, 2026.

6 land routes open to Iran: How Pak thumbed its nose at Trump's Hormuz blockadePakistan has opened six overland routes to Iran, creating a crucial workaround to Donald Trump's blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

After 31 years in jail, Rajiv Gandhi assassination convict becomes High Court lawyerOnce convicted in former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi's assassination case, AG Perarivalan, freed in 2022 after 31 years in prison, has begun a new chapter as a practising lawyer at the Madras High Court.

Live: Trump claims Iran in state of collapse, says Tehran wants Strait of Hormuz reopenedUS-Iran War Live Updates: Iran's diplomatic and military standoff with the United States has entered a more complex phase, with parallel moves on negotiation, escalation and global positioning. Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held high-level talks in St Petersburg with Vladimir Putin and Sergey Lavrov. Moscow has signalled readiness to mediate, attempting to reassert influence in the Middle East as tensions widen. At the same time, Washington is weighing its next steps. Donald Trump has reviewed a fresh Iranian proposal with top security aides, even as earlier talks stalled. The proposal reportedly touches on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and delaying discussions on Iran's nuclear programme, though US demands remain unchanged, particularly on oil transit and enriched uranium. Trump has also left the door open for direct engagement. On the ground, the situation remains volatile. Iran has made clear it will not return to pre-war conditions in the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, in Lebanon, fresh exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah continue. The death toll from Israeli attacks has crossed 2,500. Oil prices have climbed to recent highs following stalled negotiations, while global markets show mixed reactions. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Delhi sizzling at 43°C while Dubai is a breezy 31°C: Why is the desert colder?The IMD confirmed it was Delhi's third consecutive heatwave day, driven by persistent dry northwesterly winds, strong glare from the Sun, and clear skies across northwest India.

Someone save me: Video shows Mumbai man's relentless knife attack on guardsMira Road Stabbing: The video, filmed from above on a mobile phone, shows one of the victims screaming for help while the assailant repeatedly stabs him.

Michael, Hail Mary, The Mummy, Super Mario score in India despite Dhurandhar 2Despite the overwhelming success of Dhurandhar 2, four Hollywood films have scored at the Indian box office in the past months, drawing the audience with original and engaging content.

BJP sweeps local body polls in Gujarat; Congress, AAP wiped out Live updatesThe BJP recorded sweeping victories across Gujarat's municipal corporations, municipalities, district panchayats and taluka panchayats. The outcome reinforces its organisational reach in urban and rural local governance while Congress trails well behind.

CBSE three-language rule pushes schools towards Sanskrit as the easy defaultCBSE's three-language mandate is pushing many English-medium private schools towards Sanskrit as the default third language. The shift is exposing staffing, timetable and choice pressures even as students and parents adjust.

Airlines send fuel price SOS to government, warn of shutdown in operationsIn a letter to the Civil Aviation Ministry, the Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA), which represents carriers such as Air India, IndiGo and SpiceJet, warned that the sector is facing "extreme stress" due to soaring aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices.

Aadhaar is now linked to your Google account in India. Sort ofGoogle has joined hands with UIDAI to bring Aadhaar to Google Wallet. What this effectively means is that now not only you can link your Google account with your Aadhaar account but can also use Wallet to authenticate yourself in places where Aadhaar is demanded.

How RAW ran covert op for Sikkim's merger that even MEA top brass was unaware ofAs Sikkim marks 50 years of statehood, Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended the closing ceremony and launched projects worth over Rs 4,000 crore. The milestone turns the spotlight on the covert intelligence operation by RAW under RN Kao that helped in the former kingdom's integration with India in 1975.

More people are stopping SIPs: Should you also consider exiting?More investors are pressing pause on their SIPs as markets turn volatile. Seeing losses can make anyone rethink their strategy, but is stopping really the right move, or could it hurt your long-term plans?

Who is 'Singham' Ajay Sharma? Encounter specialist stirs storm in BengalThe entry of 'encounter specialist' Ajay Pal Sharma, an IPS officer from UP, in Bengal has created a flutter. A video of him warning supporters of a TMC candidate has gone viral. Here's the story of Ajay Sharma, who began his career as a dentist before turning into a tough and no-nonsense police officer.

Urban India's new reality? Pay Rs 600 to talk, walk and hang out with a 'friend'Want someone to play badminton with, go grocery shopping, or spend time on PlayStation? Even accompany you to the hospital for an MRI? You can now pay for companionship.

I'm going to die: Victim recounts US-returned Mumbai man's knife rampageThe attacker, a 31-year-old man named Jaib Zubair Ansari, was arrested within one-and-a-half hours of the incident. Mishra suffered serious injuries, including damage to his intestines, while Sen sustained minor injuries to his back.

Were US and UAE controlling rain over Iran? You should read thisMultiple social media accounts claimed that after Iran destroyed dozens of US and Israeli radars in the region, it has been raining heavily.

Oil, LPG tankers reaching Mumbai despite US blockade of Hormuz. Here's howOil and LPG tankers and vessels have reached Mumbai from the Gulf despite the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Experts say routes through Iranian waters and naval coordination limit the blockade's reach. We analyse whether the speculated routes are actually feasible.

sports.headline: Punjab Kings drop Shashank Singh vs RR: Injury concern or fielding fallout? IPL 2026: Punjab Kings left out Shashank Singh against Rajasthan Royals, officially citing an injury, though the decision comes amid a run of costly fielding errors.

BCCI's big NorthEast push explained: PM Modi inaugurates 6 indoor facilities BCCI has expanded into the NorthEast with six new indoor academies. The move aims to create year-round training and widen the talent base.

England name ageing squad for Women's T20 World Cup, include 18-year-old spinner England cricket team have named an experienced 15-player squad for the 2026 Women's T20 World Cup, led by Nat Sciver-Brunt with 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman as the standout pick alongside. They will play India and New Zealand in a white ball series prior to their opening World Cup game against Sri Lanka on 12 June at Edgbaston.

Virat Kohli's U19 India teammate now an IPL 2026 umpire: Who is he? IPL 2026: Virat Kohli reunited with former U19 teammate Ajitesh Argal after DC vs RCB. The now-umpire's full-circle moment has gone viral.

PSG hosts a defiant Bayern Munich in the UEFA Champions League semi-finals Paris Saint-Germain host Bayern Munich in a high-stakes UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg, with both sides eyeing a spot in the final after dominant runs this season. Bayern chase a treble under Vincent Kompany, while PSG, led by Luis Enrique, aim to continue their trophy-laden campaign after a commanding path to the last four.

Has absence from international cricket worked in Bhuvneshwar Kumar's favour? IPL 2026: Mitchell McClenaghan said Bhuvneshwar Kumar's success comes from repeating proven methods, not reinvention. The 36-year-old fast bowler is leading the wicket-takers' charts with 14 scalps in eight matches this season,

Bruno Fernandes' war cry to Manchester United team before Brentford win goes viral Manchester United edged closer to Champions League qualification with a 2–1 win over Brentford, powered by Bruno Fernandes' assist and viral rally. Fernandes' leadership and 19th assist of the season has driven United's surge under Michael Carrick to the brink of a top-three finish.

Kohli says speaking in school is awkward, but gives golden message to students Virat Kohli delivered a golden message to young cricketers in Delhi as he inaugurated a branch of his childhood coach's academy on Tuesday, April 28.

Even in IPL rush, Virat Kohli makes time for childhood coach, inaugurates his academy Virat Kohli stayed back in Delhi to inaugurate his childhood coach Rajkumar Sharma's new academy branch at DPS RK Puram on Tuesday, highlighting their long-standing bond. He will travel to Ahmedabad for Royal Challengers Bengaluru's next match against Gujarat Titans on Thursday.

Manchester United close to Champions League spot, but still work to do: Carrick Manchester United interim manager Michael Carrick remains grounded despite his side edging closer to securing a spot in next year's Champions League. United need just two more points to secure the spot after their 2-1 win over Brentford.

Mind your business: Kris Srikkanth grills Hemang Badani over Fleming-Dhoni jibesIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Kris Srikkanth tore into Hemang Badani after Delhi Capitals' nine-wicket loss to Royal Challengers Bengaluru, citing his old remarks on Stephen Fleming. The attack intensified scrutiny of Delhi's tactics, team calls and familiar mid-season slide after three straight defeats.

How critics made Shreyas solve his short-ball issue: Wanted to prove them wrongShreyas Iyer said criticism over his short-ball weakness pushed him to improve as the PBKS captain opened up on fixing the issue and becoming one of IPL's best finishers.

Off target: Internet erupts over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi question to Manu BhakerAt an event in New Delhi celebrating 75 years of Indian shooting excellence, Manu Bhaker was asked about cricket prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. While Bhaker's response was a masterclass in maturity, the question itself has drawn sharp criticism, reigniting the debate over the Indian media's enduring obsession with cricket.

Wrestling Federation deliberately blocking my return: Vinesh PhogatVinesh Phogat claimed the Wrestling Federation of India is deliberately trying to stop her comeback by blocking her National Open Ranking Tournament registration ahead of the Asian Games trials.

IPL 2026, PBKS vs RR preview: Iyer's unbeaten Punjab face Sooryavanshi stormPBKS bring an unbeaten run and record-breaking batting into a high-scoring clash vs RR. All eyes, though, remain on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's red-hot form.

Sindhu's heartbreaking loss triggers India's Uber Cup exit, blanked 0-5 by ChinaPV Sindhu lost to Wang Zhiyi in a thrilling three-game contest as India suffered a 0-5 defeat to China and was eliminated from the Uber Cup 2026. Sindhu squandered the win against Wang, despite leading in the decider.

The Krunal Pandya bouncer: How RCB spinner went back in time to save his futureIPL 2026: Krunal Pandya's unlikely bouncer is unsettling batters in a league tilted heavily towards power-hitting. By revisiting instincts forged on matting wickets in his village, he has reinvented himself for T20 cricket's unforgiving present.

IPL Play of the Day: Saturday night's 264 hangover drowns DC on windy MondayThree days, two heartbreaking losses. Delhi Capitals have lost their way in the Indian Premier League. How did the team manage to get bowled out for just 75 runs at their own home? We discuss in the IPL Play of the Day.

Mitchell Santner ruled out of IPL 2026 as troubles pile up for Mumbai IndiansMumbai Indians have been dealt a major setback with Mitchell Santner ruled out of IPL 2026 after suffering a left shoulder injury during their match against Chennai Super Kings at the Wankhede Stadium on April 24.

How bad was Delhi Capitals' defeat against RCB? Explained in numbersIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Rajat Patidar's Royal Challengers Bengaluru hammered the Delhi Capitals by nine wickets in what was a one-sided contest on Monday, April 27 at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

India get reality check before Women's T20 World Cup as South Africa win series 4-1SAW vs INDW, 5th T20I: India suffered another batting debacle as Laura Wolvaardt's South Africa won by 23 runs in Benoni on Monday, April 27 to win the five-match series 4-1.

DC vs RCB: Hazlewood-Bhuvi storm leaves Delhi Capitals in the dustIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: A devastating new-ball blitz from Josh Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar Kumar dismantled Delhi Capitals for just 75, before a literal dust storm and a cameo from Virat Kohli saw RCB gallop to a dominant 9-wicket victory on Monday.

Axar Patel clueless on DC's IPL slump: Even I don't understand what's happeningDelhi Capitals captain Axar Patel said that he had no clue why his team had suddenly dropped form in the Indian Premier League. DC were blown away by RCB at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.

Virat Kohli in a league of his own, completes 9000 IPL runs in styleIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Virat Kohli became the first batter to score 9000 runs in the tournament's history when Royal Challengers Bengaluru faced Delhi Capitals on April 27 at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi turning heads in Pakistan: Does he have AI chip in his bat?Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's heroics in the Indian Premier League have turned heads in Pakistan as well. Amid the flood of reactions to the teenage sensation's fearless batting, one of Pakistan's well-known cricket YouTubers even joked about whether Vaibhav has an AI chip installed in his bat.

49 stays safe: Delhi Capitals fans celebrate as DC avoid embarrassing IPL recordIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Delhi Capitals fans celebrated after the team avoided registering the lowest total in the tournament's history during their match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in New Delhi.

Uno reverse on Rinku: Mohsin gets KKR star's bat after Lucknow heroicsAfter Kolkata Knight Riders' super over win over Lucknow Super Giants, Mohsin Khan asked Rinku Singh for one of his bats. Following some help from Rishabh Pant, the LSG pacer was able to find success.

13 for 6: Delhi Capitals record worst-ever powerplay in IPL historyDelhi Capitals recorded the worst ever powerplay in the history of the Indian Premier League. Playing against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at home, Delhi put up a horror show with the bat.

My kids call him Shreyas Bhaiya: Preity Zinta praises Iyer's warm sidePunjab Kings co-owner Preity Zinta praised Shreyas Iyer as an inspiring and strategic leader, while also revealing her children share a special bond with him, lovingly calling him Shreyas Bhaiya.

2 days after 152, KL Rahul fails in Delhi, falls prey to red-hot Josh HazlewoodKL Rahul fell for one as Delhi Capitals suffered a brutal top-order collapse against the visitors. Two days after his 152, Rahul fell to Josh Hazlewood as DC crumbled in-front of the new ball attack of RCB.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
